entries.title: Procedure if Chips are Detected and I or if the "MGB P" and I or "MGB TEMP" Warning Lights Come on - TGB / MGB - Unscheduled Inspections
notices: WARNING: Lights Come on - TGB / MGB - Unscheduled Inspections en) 2025.03.12 AS350 B2, B3 Information added No A. Applicable Documents
WARNING: PERFORM THIS PROCEDURE CAREFULLY. IF THE SELF-SEALING VALVE DOES NOT CLOSE CORRECTLY, A FLOW OF HOT OIL CAN BE DANGEROUS. BE READY TO INSTALL THE MAGNETIC PLUGS. AMM 05-50-00,6-1 B2,B3
jobSetupActions: CAUTION IF THE TGB AND / OR MGB IS REMOVED TO BE SENT TO A LEVEL D WORKSHOP, SYSTEMATICALLY ATTACH THE CORRECTLY COMPLETED "PARTICLE DETECTION" TRACKING SHEET ( MTC 20-08-01-601 ) TO THE EQUIPMENT LOG CARD (FME), EVEN IF NO PARTICLES WERE DETECTED SINCE THE LAST OVERHAUL.
For the magnetic plug and the chip detector of the MGB: install the maintenance platform [ 703A95-0300-OO ] open the MGB cowlings.
procedureActions: ARNING PERFORM THIS PROCEDURE CAREFULLY. IF THE SELF-SEALING VALVE DOES NOT CLOSE CORRECTLY, A FLOW OF HOT OIL CAN BE DANGEROUS. BE READY TO INSTALL THE MAGNETIC PLUGS.

NOTE It is mandatory to recover all the magnetic particles found in the different locations of the MGB and TGB to count them correctly. The "Q T" is the quantity of particles found on the chip detector(s) and on NR sensor before or after the test flights; plus the quantity of particles found during operations to pass oil through a sieve and the particles collected in the housing.
When the "MGB CHIP" indicator light on the warning panel comes on

Remove the MGB magnetic plug (MGB housing) and the chip detector (conical housing). Inspect the MGB magnetic plug and the chip detector ( AMM 60-00-00, z2 If there are particles on the chip detector, remove and inspect the NR sensor ( AMM 62-31-00 4-2
a. If there are no metal particles on the MGB magnetic plug and the chip detectors, perform the fault isolation procedure on the detection system ( AMM 60- 00-00,1-1

b. If there are metal particules on the MGB, perform the following procedure. (1) Method to collect particles Drain the MGB oil ( AMM 12-10-00,3-1 ) and pass the oil through a sieve to collect the particles in suspension. Collect the particles retained through the chip detector and the magnetic plug and the NR sensor. Collect particles from the accessible parts of the MGB using a magnetic pin introduced through the different openings. Wash and recover the particles retained in the MGB oil cooling system.

NOTE If there is significant contamination, replace the MGB oil cooler ( AMM 79- 21-00,4-1 If possible, open the oil filter and collect the particles retained inside it. Identify and count the particles collected ( MTC 20-08-01-601 ). (2) Interpretation of the results (a) If "QT" is less than the criteria defined in ( MTC 20-08-01-601 perform the following additional operations.
Install the lower magnetic plug and the upper chip detector and, if necessary, install the NR sensor ( AMM 62-31-00,4-3 Fill the MGB with oil (AMM

Perform a hover flight, at the maximum weight, for 10 minutes ( FLM Section 8 ). Identify and count the particles collected on the chip detector and the magnetic plug as per ( MTC 20-08-01-601 If "QT" is less than the criteria defined in MTC 20-08-01-601 perform a special monitoring procedure: ALF check of the magnetic plug as per ( ) over a period equal MTC 20-08-01-601 to the number of flight hours performed between the last check of the magnetic plug without detection of particles and the detection event. Example: - check of the magnetic plug at 100 flight hours inspection without detection = TO, - detection of particles following MGB Chip coming on: 60 flight hours after TO, - short interval monitoring (check of magnetic plug) is performed at the ALF check for 60 flight hours. At the end of this special monitoring period, if there are particles on the chip detector, remove and inspect the NR sensor. If during the entire duration of this special monitoring, "QT" is less than the criteria defined in ( MTC 20-08-01-601 ), continue with the normal inspection intervals specified in ( MSM 05-21-00 If "QT" exceeds the criteria defined in ( MTC 20-08-01-601 ) and if there is a large quantity of particles on the chip detector and/or the magnetic plug, perform the procedures in the following paragraphs. (b) If "QT" exceeds the criteria defined in ( MTC 20-08-01-601 ) and if there is a large quantity of particles on the chip detector and / or the NR sensor: remove the rotor mast ( AMM 62-31-00,4-1 ) and disassemble it ( AMM 62-31-00,4-2 check the condition of the rotor mast bearings, replace the malfunctioning components, assemble the rotor mast ( ) and install it on AMM 62-31-00,4-3 the MGB ( AMM 62-31-00,4-1 (c) If "QT" exceeds the criteria defined in ( MTC 20-08-01-601 ) and if there is a large quantity of particles on the magnetic plug: remove the MGB ( AMM 63-21-00,4-1 remove the oil pump ( AMM 63-21-00,4-4 epicyclic reduction gear: check the bearings and the planet and sun gear pinions. If anomalies are found, change the epicyclic reduction gear ( AMM 62-31-00,42 ) and ( AMM 62-31-00,4-3 bevel reduction gear: perform a search for particles in the bevel ring gear through the filler cap, check the condition of the teeth of the bevel gear, perform a tactile check of the bearings of the MGB input pinion, if anomalies are found, replace the MGB, MGB oil pump: check the condition of the bearings of the vertical shaft, check the teeth of the MGB oil pump, if anomalies are found, replace the MGB oil pump, install the oil pump ( AMM 63-21-00,4-4 install the MGB ( AMM 63-21-00,4-2
When the "TGB CHIP" indicator light on the warning panel comes on
Remove the TGB magnetic plug. Inspect the T GB magnetic plug.
a. If there are no metal particules, perform the fault isolation procedure on the detection system ( AMM 60-00-00, 1-1

b. If there are no metal particles, perform the following procedure. (1) Method to collect particules Drain the TGB oil (AMM ) and pass the oil through a sieve. Collect the particles retained through the TGB magnetic plug. Collect the particles from the accessible parts of the bottom of the TGB using a magnetic pin introduced through the TGB oil filler cap. Identify and count the particles collected ( MTC 20-08-01-601 (2) Interpretation of the results (a) If "QT" exceeds the criteria defined in ( MTC 20-08-01-601 replace the TGB ( AMM 65-21-00,4-1 ) and ( AMM 65-21-00,4-2 (b) If "QT" is less than the criteria defined in ( MTC 20-08-01-601 perform the following operations.
Install the TGB magnetic plug. Fill the TGB with oil (AMM 12-10-00,3-1
Perform a hover flight, at the maximum weight, for 10 minutes ( FLM Section 8 ).

Perform an inspection of the TGB magnetic plug ( MTC 20-08- 01-601 If "QT" exceeds the criteria defined in ( MTC 20-08-01-601 replace the TGB ( AMM 65-21-00,4-1 ) and ( AMM 65-21-00,4-2 If "QT" is less than the criteria defined in ( MTC 20-08-01-601 ) or if the quantity of particles collected on the TGB magnetic plug is very small, perform a special monitoring procedure: ALF check of the magnetic plug as per ( MTC 20-08-01-601 ) over a period equal to the number of flight hours performed between the last check of the magnetic plug without detection of particles and the detection event. Example: - check of the magnetic plug at 100 flight hours inspection without detection = TO, - detection of particles following TGB Chip coming on: 60 flight hours after TO, - short interval monitoring (check of magnetic plug) is performed at the ALF check for 60 flight hours. If during the entire duration of this special monitoring "Q T" is less than the criteria defined in ( MTC 20-08-01-601 ), continue with the normal inspection intervals for the TGB magnetic plug specified in ( MSM 05-21-00
When the "MGB TEMP" indicator light comes on a

Check the MGB oil level If the level is normal, perform the check of the detection system ( AMM 63- 42-00,1-1 If the level is low, locate the leak and repair or replace the MGB ( AMM 63- 63-21-00,42 B2-B3 Rev.022. Fill the MGB with oil (AMM 12-10-00,3-1

entries.title: Steps to be Taken on Aircraft Struck by Lightning - Unscheduled Inspections
notices: CAUTION: PRIOR TO START WORK, READ THE GENERAL INSTRUCTIONS CONCERNING THE NAVIGATION SYSTEMS ( AMM 34-00-00,3-1
criteria: Steps to be Taken on Aircraft Struck by Lightning - Unscheduled Inspections AMM 05-50-00,6-10 REFERENCE Documentary Unit References/Effectivity Language Issue Date EFFECTIVITY Model Version Serial Number STATUS Type of modification Description Reason for update Maintenance work affected AMM 05-50-00,6-10 Steps to be Taken on Aircraft Struck by Lightning - Unscheduled Inspections en) 2010.02.04 AS350 B2 New task Yes A. Applicable Documents A.I. Main information AMM 34-00-00,3-1 AMM 60-00-00,6-2 A.2. Conditional information General Safety Instructions - Navigation Check - Chips Detector Removal - MGB Removal - Forward Shaft Sectiont Removal - Rear Shaft Section Removal - TGB AMM 63-21-00,4-1 AMM 65-11-00,4-1 AMM 65-11-00,4-5 AMM 65-21-00,4-1 A.3. General information MTC 20.08.04.601 B. Special Tools Commercial magnetization measurement equipment: MDCR "Fluotest Tur Elec", manufactured by S.R.E.M. AMM 05-50-00,6-10 82 C. Materials None D. Routine

Replacement Parts None E. Procedure figure 1: Steps to be Taken on Aircraft Struck by Lightning E.I. Lightning strike circumstances a. Write a report on the circumstances of the in-flight or on-ground lightning strike and indicate the following: (1) The flight conditions (airspeed, altitude, weather conditions). (2) The flight maneuvers and phases carried out during and after the lightning strike: (a) torque level and time spent at this level, (b) number of actions on the rudder pedals and amplitudes of such actions, (c) if one or several autorotations were carried out, and at which NR level. (3) The flying time after the lightning strike. (4) Any particular facts which occurred during or after the lightning strike. b. For the systems, especially indicate the following: (1) total absence of disturbance during and/or after the lightning strike or, otherwise, give a description of the disturbance and damage found. E.2. Definition of the damage a. Metallic mechanical parts (MRH - TRH - Drive systems) (1) Burn or melting marks (arc strike): (a) in the ball joints, (b) in the bearings (balls, rollers, bearing raceways), (c) on the gear teeth. (2) Melting or stripping of the bonding braids. b. Metallic structures (Fuselage, stabilizer). (l) Vaporization. (2) Burns. (3) Melting. c C0mposite structure (l) Burns. (2) Delamination. (3) Lifting, melting of electrical bonding components. (4) Flaked finish paint. d. External equipment (l) Damage. AMM 05-50-00,6-10 82 (2) Destruction. E.3. Post-flight checks CAUTION PRIOR TO START WORK, READ THE GENERAL INSTRUCTIONS CONCERNING THE NAVIGATION SYSTEMS ( AMM 34-00-00,3-1 CAUTION CHECK THE JUNCTIONS OF ALL THESE ITEMS WITH THE SUB ASSEMBLY OR SUB-ASSEMBLIES TO WHICH THEY ARE SECURED. a. Inspect for signs of lightning strike on all the external parts of the aircraft, and in particular: (1) the main rotor blades and the tail rotor blades, (2) the main rotor hub (MRH) and the tail rotor hub (TRH), (3) the landing gear, (4) the vertical stabilizers, (5) the horizontal stabilizer, (6) the fuselage elements, especially at section junctions (rivets, splices), (7) the mechanical and electrical external equipment (hoist, sling, cable cutter, emergency floatation gear, rear-view mirrors, windshield wipers, Pitot heads, lights, antennas, loudspeakers, tail bumper, etc.). b. Check the flight controls for rough points. c. Check the mechanical and electrical equipment for correct operation, in particular the magnetic and gyro-magnetic compasses. In case of doubt, carry out a compensation ( MTC 20.08.04.601 AMM 05-50-00,6-10 82 E.4. Steps to be taken with the rotor blades a. Any blade (either a main or a tail rotor blade) which suffered lightning strike must be returned to the manufacturer. E.5. Steps to be taken with the transmission assemblies NOTE For the magnetization measurements, the check for condition and the follow-up of the magnetic chip detectors, the removals and particular checks, the sensitive and visual check of the tail rotor drive bearings, send a report to EUROCOPTER Customer Support Department. Write "LIGHTNING STRIKE INCIDENT" on the log card of the items or equipment removed and returned to the manufacturer (MRH, MGB, tail rotor drive shaft, TGB, TRH, etc.). a. Main rotor hub and epicyclic gear module NOTE

If an anomaly is detected, return the item in question to the manufacturer. (1) Check the magnetic chip detector ( AMM 60-00-00,6-2 (a) Criteria: return the assembly to the manufacturer if metal chips are detected, if there are no metal chips, carry out a close monitoring of the magnetic chip detector as per the frequencies used when the assembly was put into service. If no defects are found during these checks, resume the normal maintenance cycle. (2) Inspect the bonding braids for condition. (3) Inspect all the junction areas which exhibit electrical discontinuity: (a) blades/hub: attachment bushings, blade attachment pins, AMM 05-50-00,6-10 82 (b) ball ends of rods and their junctions (pitch horns and swashplates), (c) bearings: check the raceways and the rolling elements, for the removable bearings, (d) check for rough points on the non-removable bearings, (e) elastomer parts (spherical thrust bearings, frequency adapters), (f) servo-controls (ball ends, rods and bodies), (g) detailed visual inspection of the teeth of the planet gears, fixed ring gear and sun gear (tooth flank and root). b. Tail gearbox and tail rotor hub (1) If the tail rotor blades and/or the TRH are damaged: (a) remove the T GB and the TRH ( AMM 65-21-00,4-1 (b) return the items to the manufacturer. c Main gearbox (1) Check the magnetic chip detector ( AMM 60-00-00 6-2 (a) Criteria: return the assembly to the manufacturer in metal chips are detected , if there are no metal chips, carry out a close monitoring of the magnetic chip detector as per the frequencies used when the assembly was put into service. If no defects are found during these checks, resume the normal maintenance cycle. (2) Inspect the following items for condition: the MGB suspension bars, the ball Pints, the fittings and the MGB-to-airframe attachment. AMM 05-50-00,6-10 B (3) Carry out a detailed visual inspection of the engine-to-MGB coupling (flexible couplings and shafts). (4) Carry out the magnetization measurements with the tool Magnetization measurement equipment: MDCR "Fluotest Tur Elec", manufactured by S.R.E.M. on points (1) , (2) and (3) , and contact EUROCOPTER Customer Support Department or If the following is detected: arc strike on one of the inspected items, a defect during the inspection of the epicyclic gear module. Remove the MGB ( AMM 63-21-00,4-1 (c) Remove the damaged items and return them to the manufacturer. d' Tail rotor drive: (1) Carry out a detailed visual inspection of the components of the tail rotor drive: (a) shafts, (b) flanges, (c) flexible couplings, (d) bearings. (2) Carry out a sensitive check of the bearings. NOTE If there are no defects found (damage, signs of overheating), carry out a close monitoring of the bearings as per the frequencies used when the assembly was put into service. If no defects are found during these checks, resume the normal maintenance cycle. B2-B3 Rev 22.00 AMM 05-50-00,6-10 82 (a) Criteria: if a defect is detected during the sensitive and visual check of the bearings, or if signs of arc strike are found on one of the components of the tail rotor drive. (b) Remove the tail rotor drive assembly ( ) and ( AMM 65-11-00,4-1 AMM 65-11-00,4-5 ) and send it to the manufacturer for expert analysis: shafts, coupling flanges, bearings, flexible couplings. E.6. Steps to be taken with the structure / equipment items NOTE In case of damage, contact EUROCOPTER Customer Support Department. a. Determine the path of the current in the structure or in the vicinity of the equipment, by finding any possible damage: (1) at the junction of the component (for example: stabilizer, landing gear, equipment, etc.) on the main structure, (2) at the inner junctions of a component; for example, for a landing gear, these are the bolts which attach it onto the airframe, the interfaces of the sliding parts, (3) at the other junctions, especially: frame/fuselage interfaces, attachments of transmission assemblies, at the cabin floor and the transmission deck, attachments of external equipment: mechanical equipment (hoist, tail bumper, etc.) and electrical equipment (antennas, lights, etc.), attachments of structural elements: landing gear, stabilizer, fin. AMM 05-50-00,6-10 82 O o Steps to be Taken on Aircraft Struck by Lightning Figure 1/1 C3500550A000100MUOO AMM 05-50-00,6-10 82 AMM 05-50-00,6-10 82

Inspection criteria: Replacement Parts None E. Procedure figure 1: Steps to be Taken on Aircraft Struck by Lightning E.I. Lightning strike circumstances a. Write a report on the circumstances of the in-flight or on-ground lightning strike and indicate the following: (1) The flight conditions (airspeed, altitude, weather conditions). (2) The flight maneuvers and phases carried out during and after the lightning strike: (a) torque level and time spent at this level, (b) number of actions on the rudder pedals and amplitudes of such actions, (c) if one or several autorotations were carried out, and at which NR level. (3) The flying time after the lightning strike. (4) Any particular facts which occurred during or after the lightning strike. b. For the systems, especially indicate the following: (1) total absence of disturbance during and/or after the lightning strike or, otherwise, give a description of the disturbance and damage found. E.2. Definition of the damage a. Metallic mechanical parts (MRH - TRH - Drive systems) (1) Burn or melting marks (arc strike): (a) in the ball joints, (b) in the bearings (balls, rollers, bearing raceways), (c) on the gear teeth. (2) Melting or stripping of the bonding braids. b. Metallic structures (Fuselage, stabilizer). (l) Vaporization. (2) Burns. (3) Melting. c C0mposite structure (l) Burns. (2) Delamination. (3) Lifting, melting of electrical bonding components. (4) Flaked finish paint. d. External equipment (l) Damage. AMM 05-50-00,6-10 82 (2) Destruction. E.3. Post-flight checks CAUTION PRIOR TO START WORK, READ THE GENERAL INSTRUCTIONS CONCERNING THE NAVIGATION SYSTEMS ( AMM 34-00-00,3-1 CAUTION CHECK THE JUNCTIONS OF ALL THESE ITEMS WITH THE SUB ASSEMBLY OR SUB-ASSEMBLIES TO WHICH THEY ARE SECURED. a. Inspect for signs of lightning strike on all the external parts of the aircraft, and in particular: (1) the main rotor blades and the tail rotor blades, (2) the main rotor hub (MRH) and the tail rotor hub (TRH), (3) the landing gear, (4) the vertical stabilizers, (5) the horizontal stabilizer, (6) the fuselage elements, especially at section junctions (rivets, splices), (7) the mechanical and electrical external equipment (hoist, sling, cable cutter, emergency floatation gear, rear-view mirrors, windshield wipers, Pitot heads, lights, antennas, loudspeakers, tail bumper, etc.). b. Check the flight controls for rough points. c. Check the mechanical and electrical equipment for correct operation, in particular the magnetic and gyro-magnetic compasses. In case of doubt, carry out a compensation ( MTC 20.08.04.601 AMM 05-50-00,6-10 82 E.4. Steps to be taken with the rotor blades a. Any blade (either a main or a tail rotor blade) which suffered lightning strike must be returned to the manufacturer. E.5. Steps to be taken with the transmission assemblies NOTE For the magnetization measurements, the check for condition and the follow-up of the magnetic chip detectors, the removals and particular checks, the sensitive and visual check of the tail rotor drive bearings, send a report to EUROCOPTER Customer Support Department. Write "LIGHTNING STRIKE INCIDENT" on the log card of the items or equipment removed and returned to the manufacturer (MRH, MGB, tail rotor drive shaft, TGB, TRH, etc.). a. Main rotor hub and epicyclic gear module NOTE

Inspection criteria: If an anomaly is detected, return the item in question to the manufacturer. (1) Check the magnetic chip detector ( AMM 60-00-00,6-2 (a) Criteria: return the assembly to the manufacturer if metal chips are detected, if there are no metal chips, carry out a close monitoring of the magnetic chip detector as per the frequencies used when the assembly was put into service. If no defects are found during these checks, resume the normal maintenance cycle. (2) Inspect the bonding braids for condition. (3) Inspect all the junction areas which exhibit electrical discontinuity: (a) blades/hub: attachment bushings, blade attachment pins, AMM 05-50-00,6-10 82 (b) ball ends of rods and their junctions (pitch horns and swashplates), (c) bearings: check the raceways and the rolling elements, for the removable bearings, (d) check for rough points on the non-removable bearings, (e) elastomer parts (spherical thrust bearings, frequency adapters), (f) servo-controls (ball ends, rods and bodies), (g) detailed visual inspection of the teeth of the planet gears, fixed ring gear and sun gear (tooth flank and root). b. Tail gearbox and tail rotor hub (1) If the tail rotor blades and/or the TRH are damaged: (a) remove the T GB and the TRH ( AMM 65-21-00,4-1 (b) return the items to the manufacturer. c Main gearbox (1) Check the magnetic chip detector ( AMM 60-00-00 6-2 (a) Criteria: return the assembly to the manufacturer in metal chips are detected , if there are no metal chips, carry out a close monitoring of the magnetic chip detector as per the frequencies used when the assembly was put into service. If no defects are found during these checks, resume the normal maintenance cycle. (2) Inspect the following items for condition: the MGB suspension bars, the ball Pints, the fittings and the MGB-to-airframe attachment. AMM 05-50-00,6-10 B (3) Carry out a detailed visual inspection of the engine-to-MGB coupling (flexible couplings and shafts). (4) Carry out the magnetization measurements with the tool Magnetization measurement equipment: MDCR "Fluotest Tur Elec", manufactured by S.R.E.M. on points (1) , (2) and (3) , and contact EUROCOPTER Customer Support Department or If the following is detected: arc strike on one of the inspected items, a defect during the inspection of the epicyclic gear module. Remove the MGB ( AMM 63-21-00,4-1 (c) Remove the damaged items and return them to the manufacturer. d' Tail rotor drive: (1) Carry out a detailed visual inspection of the components of the tail rotor drive: (a) shafts, (b) flanges, (c) flexible couplings, (d) bearings. (2) Carry out a sensitive check of the bearings. NOTE If there are no defects found (damage, signs of overheating), carry out a close monitoring of the bearings as per the frequencies used when the assembly was put into service. If no defects are found during these checks, resume the normal maintenance cycle. B2-B3 Rev 22.00 AMM 05-50-00,6-10 82 (a) Criteria: if a defect is detected during the sensitive and visual check of the bearings, or if signs of arc strike are found on one of the components of the tail rotor drive. (b) Remove the tail rotor drive assembly ( ) and ( AMM 65-11-00,4-1 AMM 65-11-00,4-5 ) and send it to the manufacturer for expert analysis: shafts, coupling flanges, bearings, flexible couplings. E.6. Steps to be taken with the structure / equipment items NOTE In case of damage, contact EUROCOPTER Customer Support Department. a. Replacement Parts None E. Procedure E.I. Definition: a. This incident occurs after freewheel slippage, followed by sudden reengagement. b There are two cases possible: (1) Light jerks: Torque value below the maximum transient torque, that is to say approximately 700 N (6,196 in.lb). This case concerns non-rotation of the nut of the drive gear. (2) Heavy jerks: The drive gear turns, that is to say torque value above 700 N (6, 196 in.lb). BF@Re H125- S3 0 Step? , - 2B2,B3 a Light jerks (1)

Replace the freewheel as per EMM TURBOMECA . Send the freewheel to the engine manufacturer and indicate the reason for removal. (2) Check the jerk indicator (rotation of the drive nut on the coupling sleeve). (3) Check the different sections of the tail rotor drive shaft ( AMM 65-11 00,6-1 ), ( AMM 65-11-00,6-3 ), ( AMM 65-11-00,6-10 ) and check for: distortion, condition of the paint (no flaking), condition of the rivets that attach the drive shaft flanges (no loose rivets). (4) Check the flexible couplings of the tail rotor drive ( AMM 65-11-00,6-4 and the attachment bolts ( AMM 65-11-00,6-7 In case of crack(s) or distortion(s),

discard the flexible couplings and the bolts. (5) Check the tail boom root for condition (no loose rivets, no distortions). b Heavy jerks (1) Carry out all the operations given in the steps to be taken in case of light jerks, PLUS the operations below: (2) Remove the MGB ( AMM 63-21-00,4-1 send the MGB for overhaul and indicate the reason for removal,

discard the planet gear carrier and the bevel gear / vertical shaft attachment bolts. (3) Inspect the main rotor blades (visual check and tapping check of the bonded surfaces) ( AMM 62-11-00,6-1 (4) Check the drag deflection of the main rotor blades ( AMM 62-11-00,6-2 AMM 05-50-00,6-12 B2,B3 (5) Check the following components: frequency adapters ( AMM 62-21-00,6-7 spherical thrust bearings ( AMM 62-21-00,6-4 STARFLEX star (AMM 62-21-00,6-1 the condition of the seal between the STARFLEX star and the rotor mast. (6) Inspect the components of the engine-to-MGB coupling (inspect for cracks or distortions): if crack(s) or distortion(s) is (are) found,

discard the flexible couplings, the bolts and the engine-to-MGB coupling shaft. (7) Carry out a visual check: of the MGB suspension bars ( AMM 63-32-00,6-1 the bidirectional suspension cross-member ( AMM 63-31-00,6-4 (8) If a defect is found during the inspection of the engine-to-MGB coupling, carry out an inspection for cracks (fluorescent penetrant inspection) ( MTC 20.02.09.601 ) on the following components: the lugs that attach the suspension bars to the upper casing, the fittings that attach the suspension bars to the transmission deck, the suspension bar attachment yokes. (9) If no defects are found during the inspection of the engine-to-MGB coupling, check the following components: (a) the MGB suspension bars ( AMM 63-32-00,6-1 (b) the bolts of the MGB suspension bars ( AMM 63-32-00,6-2 (10) If a defect is found during the inspection of the MGB suspension bars and bolts, carry out an inspection for cracks (fluorescent penetrant inspection) MTC 20.02.09.601 ) on the following components: the lugs that attach the suspension bars to the upper casing, the fittings that attach the suspension bars to the transmission deck, the suspension bar attachment yokes. AMM 05-50-00,6-12 B2,B3 (11) Remove/install the hub body ( ) to check the AMM 65-21-00,4-8 woodruff key of the tail rotor drive shaft for condition. AMM 05-50-00,6-12 B2,B3

entries.title: Procedure After Oil Leaks from the Gearboxes - Unscheduled Inspections
criteria: Procedure After Oil Leaks from the Gearboxes - Unscheduled Inspections AMM 05-50-00,6-14 REFERENCE Documentary Unit References/Effectivity Language Issue Date EFFECTIVITY Model Version Serial Number STATUS Type of modification Description Reason for update Maintenance work affected AMM 05-50-00,6-14 Procedure After Oil Leaks from the Gearboxes - Unscheduled Inspections en) 2010.02.04 AS350 B2, 83 New task Yes A. Applicable Documents A.I. Main information None A.2. Conditional information FLM Section 8 A.3. General information None B. Special Tools None C. Materials None AMM 05-50-00,6-14 B2,B3 D. Routine

Replacement Parts None E. Procedure El. Definitions: a Seepage: Seepage is an imperceptible leak that cannot be measured. The area in which you find seepage is "damp", but there are no oil "drops". b Doubtful leak: There are signs of a leak and the quantity of oil is not negligible (as with seepage). The oil may collect around the area but there are no other signs of a definite leak (no oil drops when the engine is stopped, no consumption that can be measured). is possibly "accumulated" seepage or oil from another source. c Definite leak: (1) The signs show clearly that there is a leak: (a) After you stop the engine, you can see an oil flow (drops, a trickle of oil). (b) Oil collects and can clearly be seen to come from a point on the gearbox. It is also clear that it is not the result of accumulated seepage. (2) These signs are confirmed by a clear increase in the oil consumption. (3) Permitted leakage criteria for the MGB and the

TGB: (a) The oil level must not decrease to become less than the minimum level: after 24 hours when the helicopter is on the ground, after approximately 1 hour when the helicopter is in flight. (b) When you add oil, the measured oil consumption must not be more than: 5 cm3 (.305 cu in) per hour of flight for the MGB, AMM 05-50-00,6-14 B2,B3 2 cm3 (.122 cu in) per hour of flight for the TGB. E.2. Procedures: a. If you find seepage: There is no special procedure: the daily checks include checks to make sure that there are no leaks from the gearboxes. But we recommend that you clean the seepage areas regularly so that the oil does not collect and give signs of a leak. b. If you find a doubtful leak: Carefully clean the area. Refer to Section 8 of the FLM Section 8 and do a ground run for approximately 15 minutes. Immediately after the ground run, examine the area. If you find a definite oil leak, do the procedure given in paragraph c). If there is no oil flow, continue flights but be very careful when you examine this area during the daily checks. c. If you find a definite leak: There are three types of procedures and each one is related to a type of leakage: CAUTION AS A GENERAL RULE, DEFINITE OIL LEAKS ARE NOT PERMITTED. (l) A leak that is more than the tolerance: (a) Type A procedure: If possible,
repair immediately. For example:
Replace the seal or the seal holder. Send the assembly back to the factory if the operator cannot

repair it. (b) Type B procedure: If necessary, the helicopter can continue to fly but you must do a check of the leak before and after each flight. During the flights, it is possible that the leak: will become more important. If it does, do the type A procedure. will become less important. If it does, do the type C procedure. AMM 05-50-00,6-14 B2,B3 CAUTION DO THE
REPAIR AS SOON AS POSSIBLE. (2) A leak that is less than the tolerance: (a) Type C procedure: It is left to the operator to decide when to do the
repair. The helicopter can fly normally but you must examine the leak carefully during each of the daily checks. AMM 05-50-00,6-14 B2,B3
exactSteps: Inspection criteria: Procedure After Oil Leaks from the Gearboxes - Unscheduled Inspections AMM 05-50-00,6-14 REFERENCE Documentary Unit References/Effectivity Language Issue Date EFFECTIVITY Model Version Serial Number STATUS Type of modification Description Reason for update Maintenance work affected AMM 05-50-00,6-14 Procedure After Oil Leaks from the Gearboxes - Unscheduled Inspections en) 2010.02.04 AS350 B2, 83 New task Yes A. Applicable Documents A.I. Main information None A.2. Conditional information FLM Section 8 A.3. General information None B. Special Tools None C. Materials None AMM 05-50-00,6-14 B2,B3 D. Routine

Inspection criteria: Replacement Parts None E. Procedure El. Definitions: a Seepage: Seepage is an imperceptible leak that cannot be measured. The area in which you find seepage is "damp", but there are no oil "drops". b Doubtful leak: There are signs of a leak and the quantity of oil is not negligible (as with seepage). The oil may collect around the area but there are no other signs of a definite leak (no oil drops when the engine is stopped, no consumption that can be measured). is possibly "accumulated" seepage or oil from another source. c Definite leak: (1) The signs show clearly that there is a leak: (a) After you stop the engine, you can see an oil flow (drops, a trickle of oil). (b) Oil collects and can clearly be seen to come from a point on the gearbox. It is also clear that it is not the result of accumulated seepage. (2) These signs are confirmed by a clear increase in the oil consumption. (3) Permitted leakage criteria for the MGB and the

Inspection criteria: TGB: (a) The oil level must not decrease to become less than the minimum level: after 24 hours when the helicopter is on the ground, after approximately 1 hour when the helicopter is in flight. (b) When you add oil, the measured oil consumption must not be more than: 5 cm3 (.305 cu in) per hour of flight for the MGB, AMM 05-50-00,6-14 B2,B3 2 cm3 (.122 cu in) per hour of flight for the TGB. E.2. Procedures: a. If you find seepage: There is no special procedure: the daily checks include checks to make sure that there are no leaks from the gearboxes. But we recommend that you clean the seepage areas regularly so that the oil does not collect and give signs of a leak. b. If you find a doubtful leak: Carefully clean the area. Refer to Section 8 of the FLM Section 8 and do a ground run for approximately 15 minutes. Immediately after the ground run, examine the area. If you find a definite oil leak, do the procedure given in paragraph c). If there is no oil flow, continue flights but be very careful when you examine this area during the daily checks. c. If you find a definite leak: There are three types of procedures and each one is related to a type of leakage: CAUTION AS A GENERAL RULE, DEFINITE OIL LEAKS ARE NOT PERMITTED. (l) A leak that is more than the tolerance: (a) Type A procedure: If possible,
Inspection criteria: repair immediately. For example:
Inspection criteria: Replace the seal or the seal holder. Send the assembly back to the factory if the operator cannot

Inspection criteria: repair it. (b) Type B procedure: If necessary, the helicopter can continue to fly but you must do a check of the leak before and after each flight. During the flights, it is possible that the leak: will become more important. If it does, do the type A procedure. will become less important. If it does, do the type C procedure. AMM 05-50-00,6-14 B2,B3 CAUTION DO THE
Inspection criteria: REPAIR AS SOON AS POSSIBLE. (2) A leak that is less than the tolerance: (a) Type C procedure: It is left to the operator to decide when to do the
Inspection criteria: repair. The helicopter can fly normally but you must examine the leak carefully during each of the daily checks. AMM 05-50-00,6-14 B2,B3

entries.title: AMM 05-50-00,6-17
procedureActions: F l Introduction a Tests condition The engine power check sequences must be run in stabilized flight, at close to maximum continuous power ( FLM Section 4.2 ). The atmosphere must be calm. Avoid any sideslip (in particular to the right).
Flushing the engine
a. Flush the engine ( EMM ).

Perform another check flight as per the Flight Manual including several engine power check sequences. (1) If the results are within tolerance limits, continue flights and perform engine power check sequences at the intervals recommended in the manufacturer's documents. (2) If the out-of-tolerance result persists despite this flushing, carry out checks of the measuring channels. AMM 05-50-00,6-17 82
Check of the measuring channels

Check the integrity of all the measuring channels required for the power check. NG (EMM). Torque . Torque transmitter ( EMM ). Torque indication channel ( AMM 77-00-00 6-1 NR indication ( AMM 76-11-01 ,5-1 Altimetric indication ( AMM 34-12-00,5-2 Outside air temperature ( AMM 31-61-00,5-3 POST MOD 073264 Outside air temperature: place a precision thermometer close to the temperature probe and check that the temperature read on the indicator corresponds to that given by the precision thermometer at 0.50C or 0.90F PRE MOD 073264
NOTE The precision of the outside air temperature has a significant impact on the final result.

b. Ensure that there is no hot air recirculating from the engine compartment in the engine air intake. To this end: (1) ensure proper seating of the seal at the air intake cowling (AMM 71-11- 00,4-5 (2) ensure that the cowlings are properly parallel ( AMM 53-51-00 4-1
Check that there are no leaks at the P2 system when it is closed.
d. Carry out fault isolation following an incorrect engine power check as described in the ( EMM ). F 4
Check flight

Perform another check flight including several engine power check sequences ( FLM Section 4.2 ). AMM 05-50-00,6-17 82 (1) If the results are within tolerance limits, continue flights and perform engine power checks at the intervals recommended in the manufacturer's documentation. (2) If the out-of-tolerance result is confirmed, despite all these checks, replace the engine as soon as possible ( AMM 71-11-00,4-1

criteria: Measures Required in Case of a Contaminated Engine Oil Circuit - Unscheduled Inspections AMM 05-50-00,6-19 REFERENCE Documentary Unit References/Effectivity Language Issue Date EFFECTIVITY Model Version Serial Number STATUS Type of modification Description Reason for update Maintenance work affected AMM 05-50-00,6-19 Measures Required in Case of a Contaminated Engine Oil Circuit - Unscheduled Inspections en) 2010.02.04 AS350 B2, B3 New task Yes A. Applicable Documents A.I. Main information EMM TURBOMECA FLM Section 8 AMM 12-10-00,3-1 AMM 79-00-00,3-1 A.2. Conditional information AMM 79-21-00,4-1 A.3. General information MTC 20.04.01=102 MTC 20.07.02.210 B. Special Tools None Filling / Draining - Gearboxes Filling / Draining - Engine Oil System Removal / Installation - AMM 05-50-00,6-19 B2,B3 Engine / MGB Oil Cooler C. Materials None D. Routine

Replacement Parts None E. Procedure NOTE Whenever oil contamination is detected, first refer to EMM TURBOMECA . Engine oil contamination may require or not the engine to be removed. In both cases, the engine oil circuit must be rinsed in accordance with the following procedure. E.I. Engine oil circuit rinsing procedure NOTE Read the savety instructions ( MTC 20.07.02.210 a. Drain the aircraft circuit ( AMM 12-10-00 3-1 b. Drain the engine (accessory drive housing, reduction gear housing, oil filter) ( EMM TURBOMECA and AMM 79-00-00,3-1 c. In case of severe clogging or contamination of the oil filter,

replace the oil cooler ( AMM 79-21-00,4-1 d. If the oil cooler is not clogged, clean the oil coolers and the oil tank ( MTC 20.04.01.102 e. Replenish the circuit ( AMM 12-10-00,3-1 f. Perform a check-out ground run ( FLM Section 8 ): for at least: > 5 minutes at a minimum oil temperature of 400 C (1040 F). g. Drain the aircraft circuit ( AMM 12-10-00,3-1 AMM 05-50-00,6-19 B2,B3 h. Drain the engine (accessory drive housing, reduction gear housing, oil filter) ( EMM TURBOMECA and AMM 79-00-00,3-1 i.

Replace the oil filter cartridge ( EMM TURBOMECA j' RePlenish the circuit ( AMM 12-10-00,3-1 NOTE The oil heater and the oil/fuel heat exchanger are part of the circuit, and must therefore be included in the checking and cleaning program. k. Refer to the engine manufacturer's documentation ( EMM TURBOMECA ) if the contamination severity requires special engine monitoring to be performed after rinsing. AMM 05-50-00,6-19 B2,B3

entries.title: Actions to be taken in the events of abnormal behaviour of helicopter on the ground, with rotor spinning - Unscheduled   Inspection Criteria - Main Rotor Blades Inspection Criteria - Starflex Assembly Inspections
procedureActions: E The steps described below are to be carried out on any helicopter manifesting a start of divergent dynamic excitation during flight or on the ground with rotor spinning. Example : dynamic phenomena occuring during a failed auto-rotation. The fatigue damage is not necessarily apparent. It is therefore advisable to know : The duration of the phenomenon. The excitation source (main or tail rotor). The stress level; to this end, an expert's analysis of the most critical element of the offending system shall be performed in order to check if its elastic limit was exceeded (residual deformation).
NOTE Under all circumstances, contact the customer service technical department for additional instructions. F l Main rotor hub
Check STARFLEX star ( AMM 62-21-00,6-1
Check the ball joint of the Starflex star ( AMM 62-21-00,6-2
Check the spherical thrust bearings ( AMM 62-21-00,6-4
Check the frequency adapters ( AMM 62-21-00,6-7 Rotor shaft
Check droop restrainer stirrup ( AMM 62-21 -OO 6-10 F 3 MGB suspension a
Check laminate stops ( AMM 63-31-00,6-1
Check MGB suspension bars ( AMM 63-32-00,6-1 F 4 Landing gear
Check the landing gears ( AMM 32-11-00,6-1
Check the landing gear attachments ( AMM 32-11-00,6-3
Check the blades and the shock absorbers ( AMM 32-11-00, A
d. Carry out the shock absorption check ( AMM 32-11-00,6-2 ) and check the shock absorbers in case of resonance. F 5 Main rotor blades
a. Visually inspect the blades and carry out a tapping test on the bonded surfaces ( AMM
Check the flapping and drag deflection of the rotor blades ( AMM 62-11-00,6-1 MTC 20-60-00-415 F 6 Tail rotor blades
Check the tail rotor blades ( AMM 64-10-00,6-1
Check the blades in the central area ( AMM 64-10-00,6-3 F 7 Main gear box a Epicyclic module
Check NR fluctuations.

entries.title: Modified content
notices: CAUTION: BEFORE STARTING ANY WORK, READ AND APPLY THE GENERAL MECHANICAL INSTRUCTIONS ( AMM 60-00-00,3-1 AMM 05-50-00,6-21A B2,B3

NOTE: Other types of equipment may exist, the user should contact the equipment manufacturer and advise Airbus Helicopters as required. Only use accessories included in the adaptation kit of the equipment used.
NOTE: The VIBREX 117 M6 and ROTORTUNER analyzers use accelerometers with 3 pin bayonet connectors. The VIBREX 2000 and CHADWICK 8500C+ analyzers use speed sensors with 4 pin connectors.
NOTE: When using the CHADWICK 8500C+ analyzer, and in order to comply with the procedure described in this Work Card, select the "MEASUREMENT ONLY" mode.
jobSetupActions: CAUTION BEFORE STARTING ANY WORK, READ AND APPLY THE GENERAL MECHANICAL INSTRUCTIONS ( AMM 60-00-00,3-1

NOTE This Work Card was written to allow fast determination of the defects which generate excessive vibrations. Different vibration measurement systems may be used, provided that results are handled in the same way as this card, that is to say: . position of sensors, measurement units used: IPS (Inch/sec), . should the unit be different, ensure conversions (speed or acceleration, inches or mm).

Wash the hydraulic equipment items to remove corrosive deposits, dirt and other contaminants, then dry with compressed air or any other means. If necessary, re lubricate (using original product). c. Inspect the components of the heating/demisting system (induction nozzle in the bottom structure to dilute the air, distribution lines). b. Blow the inside of the lines with hot air to remove all traces of liquid. F. 14. Actions to be carried out on the air conditioning system NOTE Before starting work on the air conditioning system, refer to the air conditioning general instruction ( AMM 21-00-00,3-1 a. Inspect the air conditioning system ( AMM 21-51-10,6-1 b. Return all the sensors and electrical components to the supplier for overhaul. c. Wash the components of the condenser assembly, the sand filter and the cabin and cockpit evaporator duct to remove all corrosive deposits, dirt and other contaminants, then dry with compressed air or any other means. If necessary, re lubricate (using original product). F. 15. Actions to be carried out on the compartments, cowlings, fairings, doors and floors a. Clean the cowling hinges with cleaning agent CM 208 and protect them with oil CM 162 b. Clean the hinges, articulations, guiding rails and locks with cleaning agent CM 208 c. Carry out a detailed inspection of the upper and lower cowlings. d. Carry out a detailed inspection of the bottom structure. e. Carry out a detailed inspection of the cabin, baggage compartment and cargo compartment floors. AMM 05-50-00,6-24 B2,B3 NOTE All other equipment items not mentioned in this Task but which suffered water damage, must be washed in accordance with the MTC procedures ( MTC 20.07.03.407 ) to remove all corrosive deposits, dirt and other contaminants. Then dry with compressed air or any other means and, if necessary, re lubricate (using original product). F 16. Installation a. jobSetupActions: Reminder of the general helicopter overhaul solution.

a. The general helicopter overhaul solutions are based on the following tasks: inspection ( MSM 05-22-00 (AMM 05-50-00, A AMM 05-50-00,6-6 AMM 05-50-00,6-7 (AMM 05-50-00,6-5 (AMM ), procedure after overtorque, ), procedure after main rotor blades impact, ), procedure after tail rotor blade impact, ), procedure after hard landing, ), leveling.

NOTE This task describes the maintenance actions which make it possible to repair equipment and systems do not operate as a direct consequence of a hard landing. This task does not cover the overhaul of equipment and systems: . which did not operate before the hard landing, which are directly related to the hard landing.

Perform the checks described in this Work Card and send a report of the checks and the incident to Airbus. It is mandatory to write the following remark on the documents (Technical Intervention Sheet (FIT), etc.) returned to the manufacturer with the equipment: "Equipment removed from a helicopter after a hard landing". If there is a fire, replace all the lines and wires with traces of heating in the zone exposed to excessive temperature. The following inspections are an addition to the general inspections scheduled as preparation for the basic inspection and the inspection checks following a hard landing. Do not perform the run-up scheduled as preparation for the basic inspection. In this task, only the operations related to the standard version of the helicopter and to the optional equipment items installed at the time of the hard landing must be performed. For the optional equipment items, refer to the customized documentation.
b. The inspections to be performed on the engine after a hard landing are described in the Engine Maintenance Manual.

entries.title: Steps to be taken after rotor overspeed - Unscheduled Inspections
notices: NOTE: Overspeed is defined as any incident during which the main rotor speed exceeded 430 rpm. When returning items for overhaul, specify the reason for removal.
NOTE: In case of overspeed on the ground, contact Airbus Customer Support Department.

exactSteps: Procedure: E Overspeed is defined as any incident during which the main rotor speed exceeded 430 rpm. When returning items for overhaul, specify the reason for removal.
Procedure: NOTE In case of overspeed on the ground, contact Airbus Customer Support Department.
Procedure: Case No. 1: 430 rpm < overspeed 450 rpm
Procedure: a. Main and tail rotor blades (1)
Procedure: Perform a visual and a sound check of the bonded surfaces of: (a) the main rotor blades ( AMM 62-11-00,6-1 (b) the tail rotor blades ( AMM 64-10-00,6-1
Procedure: b. Do a visual check of the sleeves, the Starflex star, the spherical thrust-bearing and the spherical thrust-bearing pins.

Procedure: NOTE For NR above 450: if the helicopter is equipped with a data recorder (HUMS or CVFDR type): find the exact NR overspeed and apply the procedure below: if the helicopter is not equipped with a data recorder (HUMS or CVFDR type): contact Airbus Customer Support Department, if the helicopter is equipped with a VEMD and if there was no desynchronisation, use the NF values (=NR) and apply the procedure below:
Procedure: Case No. 2: 450 rpm < overspeed 470 rpm a Main rotor hub (1)

Procedure: Remove the MRH ( ) for dispatch to an approved AMM 62-21-00,4-1 workshop for expert assessment. All MRH components are affected, the starflex star, spherical thrust bearings, sleeve flanges, frequency adapters, blade pins, pitch horns, lower stop support brackets, as well as all linkage components. (a)
Procedure: Remove and discard the sleeve flanges ( AMM 62-21-00,4-3 ) for the following part numbers: 350A31-1831-06 350A31-1831-07 (b)
Procedure: Discard the sleeve / spherical thrust bearing attachment bolts. (c)
Procedure: Discard the mast / hub attachment bolts. b' Tail rotor blades (l)
Procedure: Remove the tail rotor blades ( ) for dispatch to an AMM 64-10-00,4-1 approved repair workshop. (a) Appearance and ultrasound bonding check ( AMM (b)
Procedure: Check the straightness: maximum 3 mm (.118 in.). (c) DO a
Procedure: Check of the blade ( AMM 64-10-00,6-3 Main rotor blades (1) Do a visual check and sound check of the bonded areas of the main rotor blades ( AMM 62-11-00,6-1 (2)
Procedure: Measure the drag deflection of the blades ( AMM 62-11-00,6-2 d Tail rotor drive (1)

Procedure: Perform a visual inspection (general appearance) of the drive: tube / flange coupling ( AMM 65-11-00,6-1 ) and ( AMM 65-11-00,6-3 attachment of the bearings ( AMM 65-11-00, z5 condition of the flexible couplings ( AMM 65-11-00,6-4
Procedure: e. Tail rotor hub (l)

notices: NOTE: If "A + B" is 2 1: perform a general overhaul (RG) of the MGB.
procedureActions: Processing principle following an overtorque: a, Complete Table 01 and Table 02 with the accumulated time by torque field.
b. Calculate the rate of damage to the MGB based on the accumulated time. AMM 05-50-00,6-4A B3
c. Apply the measurements in Table 03 and Table 04 as per the overtorque values and calculated rate of damage to the MGB.
Recording overtorques:

a. Table of accumulated time by torque field ( Table 01 and Table 02 The tables must be completed after each flight in overtorque. Indications are provided by the VEMD on the "OVER LIMIT" page. The flight configuration in overtorque is provided by the pilot. If the pilot cannot give the flight configuration, the overtorque is entered in the forward flight table. Table 01 and Table 02 must be attached with the MGB Log Card (FM).

b. Forward flight: OVERTORQUE VEMD DISPLAY TIME BY FIELD Table 1 - Forward flight FORWARD FLIGHT "Vi" greater than 40 Kt 105 to 110 TRQ TRA tl in Min. 110 to 118 TRQ MED t2 in Min. 118 to 131 TRQ EXT OVER 131 MAX ACCUMULATED TIME BY TORQUE FIELD

c. OVER 131 MAX General overhaul of MGB if the torque exceeds 132%

entries.title: Procedure after a hard landing POST MOD 074302 - Unscheduled Inspections
jobSetupActions: The inspections looking for cracks must be performed unless differently specified ( MTC 20-02-09-101 ).
procedureActions: Definition A hard landing with power is defined as a landing performed at the moment of impact with the estimated values described below: either a load factor of 2.4 g, or a vertical speed greater than 2 m/s.
b. Two types of checks must be performed.
CAUTION IF DAMAGE IS FOUND, IT IS FORMALLY PROHIBITED TO CONTINUE FLIGHTS. REPLACE THE TAIL BOOM (AMM 53-31-00,4-1 (1)
Check following an incident (a) The purpose is to make sure that there is no damage which can affect flight safety before continuing the mission.

Perform the inspection of the lower part of the tail boom in a zone between the junction frame and the bearing No. 4 support frame, within a 450 sector on both sides of the helicopter datum line. This operation must be performed in two steps to detect any local anomalies (dents, residual bends, even slight): . visual inspection, . tactile inspection.

Make sure that the free wheel engages correctly ( AMM 05-40- 00,6-6 (2) Detailed check after return to base AMM 05-50-00,6-5B 83 (a) The purpose is to perform the detailed inspections to look for any non-visible deformations.
Perform an inspection of the equipment items and the optional equipment ( AMM 05-50-00,6-25
Perform the check of the tail rotor drive shaft alignment ( AMM 65-11-00,6-8
Perform the check of the landing gear ( AMM 32-11-00,6-1 AMM 32-11-00,6-3 ) and ( AMM 32-11-00,6-4

Hard landing after autorotation a Definition A hard landing after autorotation is defined as a landing performed at the moment of impact with the following estimated values: . either a load factor of 4 g, or a vertical speed greater than 2.5 m/s.
Perform the operations in the paragraph Hard landing with power and the following operations. (l) Crew and passenger seats
Perform a complete inspection of the seat as per the checks provided in the chapter CHECKS ( CMM 25-11-73 ).
Make sure that the energy absorption system is not distorted. If in doubt, return the seat to the supplier. (2) Tank
Make sure that there are no deformations on the tank supports and their attachments.
Check of the tension of the tank straps ( AMM ). (3) Landing gear
Check of the landing gear ( AMM 32-11-00,6-1 and ( AMM 32-11-00,6-4 AMM 32-11-00,6-3 Removal of the shock absorber for General Overhaul ( AMM 32-11- 00,4-4 (4) Structure AMM 05-50-00,6-5B 83
NOTE A deformation or a misalignment of the doors and cowlings can be caused by abnormal structural constraints. (a) Cargo compartment
Perform the check of the lining in the cargo compartment (blistering). Look for any local buckling of the stringers between the crossbeams. (b) Tail boom

discard the blade attachment pins, .

discard the sleeve / spherical thrust bearing coupling bolts. If one of the sleeves has any buckling type damage: general overhaul of the MGB. (h) Make sure that there are no deformations and / or cracks on the lower stop caliper ( AMM 62-21-00,6-1Q (i) Perform the check of the pitch horns ( AMM 62-21-00,6-12 If a pitch horn is cracked or distorted: perform a dye penetrant inspection looking for cracks on the rotor actuator attachments on the MGB housing, on the yokes and the arms of the swashplates. (j) Perform the check of the condition and the straightness of the pitch rods ( AMM 62-33-00,6-1 If a pitch rod is cracked or distorted: perform a dye penetrant inspection looking for cracks on the rotor actuator attachments on the MGB housing, on the yokes and the arms of the swashplates. (7) Main Gear Box (a) Perform a dye penetrant inspection looking for cracks ( MTC 20- 02-09-101 ) on: . the attachment yokes of the MGB suspension bars on the upper housing, . the attachment brackets of the MGB suspension bars on the transmission deck, . the MGB suspension bar end-fittings. (b) Perform the check of the MGB suspension bars ( AMM 63-32- 00,6-1 (c) Perform the check of the MGB suspension bar bolts ( AMM 63-32- 00,6-2 (d) Perform the check of the suspension crossbeam ( AMM 63-31- and the laminated stops ( AMM 63-31-00,6-1 F5 -OO,6-5B 83

If an anomaly is found during the check: . look for any cracks on the lower MGB housing. (8) Tail Gear Box (a) Look for any cracks on the front brackets of the TGB housing. (9) MGB / engine coupling (a) Look for any cracks on the coupling yoke of the MGB / torque tube. (10) Engine (a) Refer to the ( EMM ). (11) Engine supports (a) Look for any cracks on the engine suspensions. (12) Free wheel (a)
Replace the free wheel ( EMM ) and return it to the engine manufacturer indicating the reason for removal.

entries.title: Procedure if there is an impact on the main rotor blades - Unscheduled Inspections
notices: CAUTION: IF THERE IS A SIGNIFICANT IMPACT, ALSO PERFORM THE INSPECTIONS DEFINED IN THE ENGINE MAINTENANCE MANUAL ( EMM ). (1) Remove the damaged main rotor blade(s) ( AMM 62-11-00,4-1 ) for possible repair.
procedureActions: El. Definition Impact on a blade when the ROTOR IS TURNING is defined as any contact with the blade with sufficient force to cause the rotor speed to decrease suddenly. Impact on a blade when the ROTOR IS STOPPED is defined as any contact with the blade when performing an operation on the ground.
a. Light impact Any impact on the trailing edge when the rotor is turning. Any impact on the leading edge when the rotor is stopped.

b. Significant impact Any impact on the leading edge when the rotor is turning. Any impact on the trailing edge when the rotor is stopped. Any impact on the lower or upper surfaces of the blades, when the rotor is turning or is stopped. F 2. Checks a Light impact (1)
Perform the check of the damaged main rotor blade(s) ( AMM 62-11 00,6-1 (2)
Perform the check of the STARFLEX Star (AMM 62-21-oo, zl ). (3)
Perform the check of the Frequency Adapters ( AMM 62-21-00,6-7 associated with the damaged blade(s). (4)
Perform the check of the spherical bearing(s) ( AMM 62-21-00,6-4 associated with the damaged blade(s).
b. Significant impact
CAUTION IF THERE IS A SIGNIFICANT IMPACT, ALSO PERFORM THE INSPECTIONS DEFINED IN THE ENGINE MAINTENANCE MANUAL ( EMM ). (1)
Remove the damaged main rotor blade(s) ( AMM 62-11-00,4-1 ) for possible repair.
Perform a check of the drag and flapping sweep ( AMM 62-11-00,6-2 before sending the blades for repair. (2)
Perform the check of the main rotor blades that are not damaged ( AMM 62-11-00,6-1 (3)
Perform the check of the drag and flapping sweep of the main rotor blades that are not damaged ( AMM 62-11-00,6-2 (4)
Perform the check of the STARFLEX Star ( AMM 62-21-00,6-1 (5)
Perform the check of the three Frequency Adapters, especially the gap between the upper and lower edges ( AMM 62-21-00,6-7 (6)
Perform the check of the three spherical bearings ( AMM 62-21-00,6-4 (7)
Perform the check of the three sleeve cheek plates, especially the criteria following an incident ( AMM 62-21-00,64 (8)
Perform the check of the perpendicularity and the concentricity of the Mast / Hub coupling screws ( AMM 62-21-00,6-8 (9)
criteria: F. procedure El. Definition Impact on a blade when the ROTOR IS TURNING is defined as any contact with the blade with sufficient force to cause the rotor speed to decrease suddenly. Impact on a blade when the ROTOR IS STOPPED is defined as any contact with the blade when performing an operation on the ground. a. Light impact Any impact on the trailing edge when the rotor is turning. Any impact on the leading edge when the rotor is stopped. AMM 05-50-00,6-6 B2,B3 b. Significant impact Any impact on the leading edge when the rotor is turning. Any impact on the trailing edge when the rotor is stopped. Any impact on the lower or upper surfaces of the blades, when the rotor is turning or is stopped. F 2. Checks a Light impact (1) Perform the check of the damaged main rotor blade(s) ( AMM 62-11 00,6-1 (2) Perform the check of the STARFLEX Star (AMM 62-21-oo, zl ). (3) Perform the check of the Frequency Adapters ( AMM 62-21-00,6-7 associated with the damaged blade(s). (4) Perform the check of the spherical bearing(s) ( AMM 62-21-00,6-4 associated with the damaged blade(s). b. Significant impact CAUTION IF THERE IS A SIGNIFICANT IMPACT, ALSO PERFORM THE INSPECTIONS DEFINED IN THE ENGINE MAINTENANCE MANUAL ( EMM ). (1) Remove the damaged main rotor blade(s) ( AMM 62-11-00,4-1 ) for possible
repair. NOTE Perform a check of the drag and flapping sweep ( AMM 62-11-00,6-2 before sending the blades for

repair. (2) Perform the check of the main rotor blades that are not damaged ( AMM 62-11-00,6-1 AMM 05-50-00,6-6 B2,B3 (3) Perform the check of the drag and flapping sweep of the main rotor blades that are not damaged ( AMM 62-11-00,6-2 (4) Perform the check of the STARFLEX Star ( AMM 62-21-00,6-1 (5) Perform the check of the three Frequency Adapters, especially the gap between the upper and lower edges ( AMM 62-21-00,6-7 (6) Perform the check of the three spherical bearings ( AMM 62-21-00,6-4 (7) Perform the check of the three sleeve cheek plates, especially the criteria following an incident ( AMM 62-21-00,64 (8) Perform the check of the perpendicularity and the concentricity of the Mast / Hub coupling screws ( AMM 62-21-00,6-8 (9) Perform the check of the sweep of the bolts on the three spherical thrust bearings. (10) Perform the check of the visible part of the rotor mast ( AMM 62-31- 00,6-1 NOTE

If an anomaly is found during the checks, perform the following actions: return the MGB to the factory, for general overhaul (spiral bevel and epicyclic modules), return the STARFLEX Star to the factory, return the Sleeves to the factory,
discard the vertical shaft / bevel wheel attachment screws,
discard the planet gear carriers,
discard the Frequency Adapters and the spherical bearings,
discard the Mast / Hub coupling screws and the spherical thrust bearing bolts,
discard the rotor mast,

discard the Engine / MGB drive bolts and flexible couplings. AMM 05-50-00,6-6 B2,B3 (11) Perform the check of the suspension bars ( AMM 63-32-00,6-1 (12) Perform a dye penetrant inspection ( MTC 20-02-09-601 ) to find any cracks on: the suspension bar attachment tabs on the upper housing, the suspension bar attachment brackets on the transmission deck, the suspension bar attachment yokes. (13) Perform the check of the rotating and non-rotating scissors ( AMM 62- ) and ( 33-00,6-2 AMM 62-33-00,6-3 (14) Perform the check of the swashplates guide ( AMM 62-32-00,6-2 (15) Make sure that there is a seal on the mast ( AMM 62-31-00,6-4 (16) Perform the check of the swashplates spherical bearing (AMM 62-32- 00,6-3 (17) Perform the check of the three pitch horns ( AMM 62-21-00,6-12 (18) Perform the check of the three pitch rods, especially the straightness of the rod bodies ( AMM 62-33-00,6-1 NOTE If one or more pitch lever(s) or pitch rod(s) have anomalies, perform the following actions: a dye penetrant inspection ( MTC 20-02-09-601 ) to find any cracks on the attachment tabs of the three rotor actuators on the MGB and the swashplate, return the three rotor actuators to the factory,
discard the swashplates bearing,

discard the swashplates guide. (19) Perform the check of the suspension crossbeam ( AMM 63-31-00,6-4 and the laminated stops ( AMM 63-31-00,6-1 AMM 05-50-00,6-6 B2,B3 NOTE If there is deterioration of the suspension crossbeam, perform a dye penetrant inspection ( MTC 20-02-09-601 ) to find any cracks on the MGB lower housing. (20) Perform a dye penetrant inspection ( MTC 20-02-09-601 ) to find any cracks on the attachment points of the suspension crossbeam. (21) Perform a magnetic particle inspection to find any cracks of the rotor mast (contact the Airbus Customer Service department). (22) Perform a check of the straightness of the rotor mast ( AMM 62-31- 00,6-1 NOTE For a significant impact, without removal of the MGB or the main rotor mast, perform a check of the chip detectors ( AMM 60-00-00,6-2 ) and the filters during the check After the Last Flight of the Day (ALF) ( AMM 05-40- 00,6-6 ) that follows the incident. If no anomalies are found, return to the normal maintenance cycle.

jobSetupActions: c. Impact on blade with rotor turning The impact on "blade with rotor turning" is defined for any contact of one blade or both blades with a body which can apply a resistance force against the movement of the rotor (water, snow, shrubs, poles, etc.). Impact on blade with rotor not turning The impact on "blade with rotor not turning" is defined for any contact of one blade or both blades with a resisting body during maneuvers on ground with rotor not turning. Unbalance "Unbalance" can occur, for example, if there is a loss of the leading edge protection.

criteria: Steps to be Taken for Non-Rotating Blades in Gusts - Unscheduled Inspections AMM 05-50-00,6-8 REFERENCE Documentary Unit References/Effectivity Language Issue Date EFFECTIVITY Model Version Serial Number STATUS Type of modification Description Reason for update Maintenance work affected AMM 05-50-00,6-8 Steps to be Taken for Non-Rotating Blades in Gusts - Unscheduled Inspections en) 2010.02.04 AS350 B2, B3 New task Yes A. Applicable Documents A.I. Main information AMM 62-11-00,6-1 AMM 62-11-00,6-2 Rotor Blades AMM 64-10-00,6-1 AMM 64-10-00,6-2 Inspection Criteria - Main Rotor Blades Checking the Flapping and Dragwise Deflection of the Main Inspection Trailing - Tail blade Checking - Tab in rotor center zone (without removal) A.2. Conditional information None A.3. General information None B. Special Tools None AMM 05-50-00,6-8 B2,B3 C. Materials None D. Routine

Replacement Parts None E. Procedure NOTE This procedure is applicable only if the blades are not moored and when blades are subject to: Gales that are more than 40 kts. Wind gusts. An incident that causes the blades to flap too much. E l Main rotor blades a. Inspect the skin for condition, especially for cracks on the whole surface of the blade and for any possible de-bonding from station 800 to station 2400. Inspect the bushings for condition ( AMM 62-11-00,6-1 ). b. Visually check the attachment and, in particular, make sure that the blade attachment pin slides correctly. c. If no defects are detected (beyond the acceptable criteria ( AMM 62-11-00M ), the blade can be kept in service for 10 flight hours to allow return from mission. d. Then, measure the flapping and drag deflection ( AMM 62-11-00,6-2 E 2. Tail rotor a. Perform a visual check and a tapping test of the bonded surfaces ( AMM 64- 10-00,6-1 b. Check for abnormal noises at the root of each blade by coning the blades ( AMM 64-10-00,6-2 AMM 05-50-00,6-8 B2,B3 AMM 05-50-00,6-8 B2,B3

jobSetupActions: Comply with the general safety instructions for the mechanical assemblies ( AMM 60- 00-00,3-1

c. Before removing the bolts (5) and perform a check of the tightening torque (0) value. If the tightening torque is greater than 85% of the minimum tightening torque value: disconnect the pitch rod. If the tightening torque is less than 85% of the minimum tightening torque value: disconnect the pitch rod, make sure that there is no fretting on the bolts, the bores and the mating surfaces of all the assembly parts, discard all the parts which show signs of fretting, contact the Airbus Technical Support Department to report any loss of tightening torque value.
Remove the pitch horn assembly (1) AMM 64-10-00,6-10 82 AMM 64-10-00,8-24
procedureActions: Detailed Inspection Criteria - Pitch horn assembly POST MOD 076602
Detailed check of the pitch horn assembly (1)
Perform the checks as per the table below: Zone Zone A Inspect the sensitive zones Sl very carefully. Checks
Make sure that there are no impacts, scratches, corrosion or pitting. If the depth of the damage is 0.1 mm (.0039 in.): accept the part as it is.
Make sure that the protective treatment and the paint have been applied.
Make sure that there is no fretting or wear at the interfaces.
Make sure that there are no cracks. If in doubt about a crack, perform a dye penetrant inspection ( MTC 20-02-09- 101
Repair and touch-up criteria If the depth is > 0.1 mm (.0039 in.) but 0.2 mm (.0079 in.) Sand with 400-grit abrasive paper ( MTC 20-04-03-401

procedureActions: Criteria - Rotor Shaft figure 2: Inspection Criteria - Rotor Shaft
Visually check that there is no chromium blistering / scaling on the rotor shaft output housing: outside of the rotating spider sliding zone, in the rotating spider sliding zone.
NOTE Push yaw pedals fully home (with foot) on RH side and then on LH side to check the entire zone.
If chromium blistering / scaling is detected outside the rotating spider sliding zone:.
a. Break the chromium scale or blister with a bronze tool. - Inspection Criteria - Rotor Shaft
Repair Zone Outside of rotating spider sliding zone.
NOTE Table 1 Damage permitted without repair Manually clean the affected zone with an abrasive pad .

Make sure that there is no damage to the elastomer part of the spherical bearing of the pitch rod as per the procedure indicated below.
Make sure that there are no cracks, debonding or extrusions in the elastomer part of the cylindrical bearing.
Make sure that the protective treatment and the paint present.
Make sure that there are no cracks, dents, scratches, corrosion or pitting.

Discard the component if any of the following damage is present on at least one of the elastomer layers, over a circumference greater than 900 (DETAIL C): crack (DETAIL E) and (DETAIL D), debonding (DETAIL F), extrusion (DETAIL G).

If there is no fretting damage, install the screws (1) and (3) ( AMM 65-21- 00 4-12 Maximum damage which Causes Replacement There is fretting damage.

a. Position the rotor blades so that both sides of the elastomer spherical bearing of one of the two pitch change rods can be observed on sides 1 and 2 (DETAIL B).

procedureActions: EMD - fail Code 44 F l Description The three N2 (N2A, N2B and N2C) sensors measure the power turbine rotational speed. The N2A information is used for the NR/NTL indicator on the cockpit. The N2B and N2C signals are used by the FADEC for the engine control. The N2 parameter is transmitted to the VEMD through the ARINC data bus. The failure code is raised when the NF data is declared invalid or is not available on channel A of VEMD.
LRIJ associated to this failure code ARINC connection between VEMD and FADEC Analogical connection between FADEC and 22DELTA1 N2B sensor N2C sensor FADEC VEMD

procedureActions: EMD - fail Code 48 and Code 49 F l Description The FADEC sends to the VEMD through ARINC frame the Nl limitation at take-off and in flight. The VEMD adjusts this value and displays it. This maximum take-off power (PMD) or maximum continuous power (PMC) avoids an over-temperature in the engine. The limitation calculation is based upon PO H/C and TO H/C parameters. The failure code is raised when the NGMD or NGMC data is declared invalid or is not available on channel A

procedureActions: EMD - Malfunction code 121 F l Description The malfunction code is generated if the engine control is not set correctly (to OFF - IDLE - ON (FLIGHT)) after a defined time: on the ground, the flight position cannot be set, in flight, the engine remains in flight mode. After landing, the engine can only be stopped by closing the fuel flow.
LRIJ associated with this malfunction code ARINC connection between the VEMD and the FADEC. Connection between the FADEC and the engine control. Relay 69K. VEMD. AMM 71-11-00, 1-4 83 Engine control.

procedureActions: EMD - fail Code 122 F l Description The pitch potentiometer transmits the Collective pitch Position (XPC) signal to the FADEC. It is used for power anticipation (by use of XPC datum) to maintain rotor speed within an acceptable range. The failure code is raised when the potentiometer is out of range or in case of significant transit.

procedureActions: EMD - fail Code 123 El. Description The T4 sensor measures the combustion chamber temperature. This measure checks the start temperature to avoid the engine to warm too much. There are 8 sensors (1 6 thermocouples) on two harnesses. The data from 4 sensors is sent to the FADEC, and the remaining data to the VEMD. The failure code is raised only by the FADEC in case of short-circuit, open-circuit, out of range or important transit.
LRU associated to this failure code T4 sensor T4 junction and conformation box FADEC Connection between FADEC and 22DELTA1 connector

Procedure: EMD - fail Code 123 El. Description The T4 sensor measures the combustion chamber temperature. This measure checks the start temperature to avoid the engine to warm too much. There are 8 sensors (1 6 thermocouples) on two harnesses. The data from 4 sensors is sent to the FADEC, and the remaining data to the VEMD. The failure code is raised only by the FADEC in case of short-circuit, open-circuit, out of range or important transit.
Procedure: LRU associated to this failure code T4 sensor T4 junction and conformation box FADEC Connection between FADEC and 22DELTA1 connector

YES 0K NO REFER TO BOX 2 END OF PROCEDURE (FALSE ALARM) REPLACE T4 CONFORMATION BOX Is the fault message still displayed on the VEMD? NO 0K YES VEMD - fail Code 123 Figure 1/1 Sheet 1/2 C3507111A003401 AMM 71-11-00, 1-6 83 END CHECK SENSOR WIRING FROM FADEC to 22DELTA1 CONNECTOR - IS THE TEST SATISFACTORY? procedureActions: EMD - fail Code 125 El. Description The P3 sensor determines the inlet pressure of the engine combustion chamber in order to avoid the engine surge. The failure code is raised only by the FADEC in case of short-circuit, open-circuit, out of range or important transit. At start, the P3 value is compared to PO. In case of difference between these parameters, P3 would be declared failed and this failure code is raised.
